title: -Contracts: M&A Towing and City Towing for Towing Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt two Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to:
1. Execute a contract with any amendments with M&A Towing to provide towing
services for a three (3) year period from May 1, 2025, to May 1, 2028 in an
amount not to exceed $150,000, with an option to extend for two (2) additional
one (1) year terms. Rates adjusted annually in accordance with the Consumer
Price Index.
2. Execute a contract with any amendments with City Towing to provide towing
services for a three (3) year period from May 1, 2025, to May 1, 2028 in an
amount not to exceed $150,000, with an option to extend for two (2) additional
one (1) year terms. Rates adjusted annually in accordance with the Consumer
Price Index.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The FY25-26 Adopted Budget includes funding for fleet vehicle management, which will
be used to cover the cost of towing services for City vehicles within the Public Works
Department Equipment Maintenance Division (General Fund 672) budget. Additionally,
funding will be appropriated for the next three fiscal years (FY 2026 to FY 2029) in the
Equipment Maintenance Fund during the annual budget process.
sections.current_situation:
The City conducted Request for Proposals Specification No. 24-11648, with proposals
due no later than February 20, 2025. The City received three proposals in response.
The selection committee, composed of subject matter experts from the Public Works
Equipment Maintenance and Operations Divisions, evaluated the proposals.
The evaluation criteria used to rate the proposals were as follows: Project Approach
(30%), Expertise and References (15%), Costs (25%), and Ability to Perform Services
Listed in the Scope of Services (30%). M&A Towing and City Towing were selected as
the highest-scoring vendors.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Contracts: M&A Towing and City Towing for Towing Services CONSENT CALENDAR
April 29, 2025
This contract will support the City’s fleet and is a Strategic Plan Priority Project,
advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities,
and facilities.
sections.background:
M&A Towing and City Towing responded to the Request for Proposal and were selected
after submitting the highest-scoring bids. The selected vendors have the adequate staff
and equipment necessary to service the City. The new towing services will ensure clear
guidelines and standardized pricing for towing services, which is a significant expense
for the Public Works Department.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
No identified environmental sustainability or climate impacts.
sections.rationale:
The new towing services contracts will address the City’s needs, along with stabilized
costs and fees. The new contracts will clearly address the guidelines and prices for as-
needed towing. The selected tow service providers will be utilized as needed to ensure
the City has adequate resources available for unforeseen emergencies.
sections.alternatives:
None. The City does not have the resources to tow City vehicles.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, (510) 981-6629
Don Colbert, Sr. Equipment Maintenance Supervisor, (510) 981-6467
Sara Rubio, Associate Management Analyst, (510) 981-5372

Page 2
Page 3 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: M&A TOWING FOR TOWING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City conducted RFP Solicitation No. 24-11648-C with proposals due no
later than February 20, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the City received three proposals and a selection committee composed of
subject matter experts from the Public Works Equipment Maintenance Division, and the
Public Works Operations Division, met to evaluate the proposals, and
WHEREAS, M&A Towing and City Towing were selected as the highest scoring
vendors.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with M&A Towing
to provide towing services for three (3) years from May 1 2025 to May 1, 2028 for an
amount not to exceed $150,000 with the option to extend for two (2) additional one-year
terms. Rates adjusted annually in accordance with the Consumer Price Index.
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: CITY TOWING FOR TOWING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City conducted RFP Solicitation No. 24-11648-C with proposals due no
later than February 20, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the City received three proposals and a selection committee composed of
subject matter experts from Public Works Equipment Maintenance Division, and the
Public Works Operations Division, met to evaluate the proposals, and
WHEREAS, M&A Towing and City Towing were selected as the highest scoring
vendors.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with City Towing
to provide towing services for three (3) years from May 1, 2025 to May 1, 2028 for an
amount not to exceed $150,000 with the option to extend for two (2) additional one-year
terms. Rates adjusted annually in accordance with the Consumer Price Index.
